commit.message:
fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

commit.message:
fix: always increment fetch version to prevent stale people-fetch overwriting project results

When navigating from a people detail page to a project detail page while
already in detail view, the effect fired twice — once with the stale
viewMode='people' and once with the correct viewMode='projects'. Because
the fetch version wasn't incremented (layoutView was already 'detail'),
the stale-check let the people fetch overwrite the project fetch result
with 'Profile not found', and that error persisted across all pages.

* fix(buddy): use variable-based dynamic imports for optional LLM SDKs

Use module path variables instead of string literals in dynamic import()

peer dependencies (@anthropic-ai/sdk, openai). This fixes the CI

* fix(buddy): fix timeout detection and conversation history ordering

- Timeout detection: check error name for 'Timeout' substring and
  message for 'timed out' in addition to 'timeout', matching the
  actual error classes from Anthropic SDK (APIConnectionTimeoutError)
  and OpenAI SDK. Without this, provider timeouts bypassed detection
  and were re-thrown as raw 500 errors instead of 504.

- History query: change ORDER BY from ASC to DESC with LIMIT 20, then
  reverse the result. The previous ASC+LIMIT returned the 20 oldest
  messages, so once a conversation exceeded 20 messages the user's
  latest message was excluded from the LLM context.

* fix(ai-buddy): resolve SuggestionFeedback enum deduplication conflict

The openapi-typescript generator with --dedupe-enums merges identical inline
enums, causing SpacesModuleSuggestionFeedbackFeedback to be replaced by
BuddyModuleSuggestionFeedbackFeedback. Remove duplicate enum from buddy
constants (reuse from spaces module) and update admin composable import.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J5WR2WrnA6JLDMM6RjCsad

* fix(ai-buddy): fix expired entry dedup, TOCTOU race, and cross-midnight centroid

Three bugs in the suggestion engine:

1. hasSuggestionForPattern did not check expiresAt, so expired entries in the
   map (awaiting 60s cleanup) blocked new suggestions from being created while
   getActiveSuggestions correctly filtered them out — leaving zero visible
   suggestions for an active pattern.

2. generateSuggestions had a TOCTOU race: the sync dedup check passed for two
   concurrent callers, both yielded on await resolveSpaceName, then both stored
   separate suggestions for the same pattern. Fixed with a boolean generation
   lock that causes the second caller to return early.

3. clusterByTimeOfDay computed the centroid as an arithmetic mean of
   minuteOfDay values, which is wrong for cross-midnight clusters (e.g.
   [23:50, 00:10] averaged to 12:00 PM instead of ~00:00). Fixed with
   circular statistics (atan2 of mean sin/cos on a 1440-minute circle).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J5WR2WrnA6JLDMM6RjCsad

* fix(ai-buddy): use schema-derived feedback type and PATTERN_LOOKBACK_DAYS constant

Two fixes:

1. Admin composable used BuddyModuleSuggestionFeedbackFeedback (a buddy
   module type) to cast the feedback value sent to the Spaces module API
   endpoint. This cross-module type dependency was fragile — if the buddy
   module is removed or the --dedupe-enums order changes, the import breaks.
   Now derives the type from SchemaSpacesModuleSuggestionFeedback['feedback'],
   which always resolves correctly regardless of deduplication.

2. Confidence formula used hardcoded 7 instead of PATTERN_LOOKBACK_DAYS.
   If the lookback window changes, the confidence normalization would
   silently diverge. Now uses the constant directly.

* fix(panel): fix ghost messages, scroll behavior, and suggestion animation

1. sendMessage ghost message: when the API returns non-200 or an
   unexpected data shape, the optimistic pending_* user message was
   never removed. Now falls through to fetchConversationMessages to
   reconcile state.

2. _sendMessage mounted check: _scrollToBottom was called after
   await without checking mounted, risking access to an already-
   disposed ScrollController. Added mounted guard matching the
   existing pattern in _initializeConversation.

3. _scrollToBottom on every rebuild: the Consumer builder called
   _scrollToBottom whenever any message existed, overriding the
   user's scroll position on every state change. Now tracks
   _lastMessageCount and only scrolls when it increases.

4. Suggestion card exit animation: submitSuggestionFeedback removed
   the suggestion from the list and called notifyListeners immediately,
   causing the Consumer to rebuild and dispose the card widget ~50ms
   into its 300ms animation. Decoupled removal from the API call:
   submitSuggestionFeedback now returns true without mutating the list,
   and the card calls onAnimationComplete after the animation finishes
   to trigger removeSuggestion.

* fix(panel): decouple provider check, fix WS dedup, fix disposal order

1. isProviderNotConfigured string coupling: the service compared the
   repository's error string against the exact literal produced by
   _parseError, silently breaking if the wording changed. Replaced
   with a dedicated _isProviderNotConfigured boolean flag set by
   _parseError when status is 503.

2. WebSocket message duplicate-drop: _handleMessageReceived defaulted
   a missing message_id to '', so all subsequent ID-less messages
   matched the first and were silently dropped. Now generates a
   unique synthetic ID (ws_<timestamp>) for ID-less payloads and
   only deduplicates when the server provided a real ID.

3. Disposal order: BuddyRepository was disposed before BuddyService
   in _unregisterModulesIfNeeded. BuddyService.dispose() calls
   removeListener on the repository, which throws a debug assertion
   on an already-disposed ChangeNotifier and prevents super.dispose()
   from running. Swapped the order so BuddyService disposes first.

* fix(panel): reset _isProviderNotConfigured at start of every API call

Once a 503 set _isProviderNotConfigured to true, no success path ever
reset it to false. Subsequent successful API calls (e.g. after an admin
configures the provider) cleared _error but left the flag true,
permanently disabling the chat input with "AI provider not configured"
until app restart.

Now every API method resets _isProviderNotConfigured = false alongside
_error = null at entry, so the flag is only true while the most recent
error was actually a 503.

* fix(panel): move scroll side effect out of Consumer.builder into listener

The Consumer.builder callback was mutating _lastMessageCount and

* fix(panel): store BuddyService ref to avoid context.read on defunct element

context.read<BuddyService>() in dispose() and the listener callback
throws "Looking up a deactivated widget's ancestor is unsafe" because
the element is defunct by that point. This leaked the listener on every
drawer open/close cycle, and the leaked callback would repeatedly error
on subsequent BuddyService notifications. Fixed by capturing the
BuddyService reference in a late final field during initState and
reusing it in the listener and dispose.

* fix(panel): add assistant message to list before reconciliation fetch

populate the list. If that GET failed, the AI response was silently
discarded. Now the assistant message is added to _messages immediately
after parsing, and the reconciliation fetch is wrapped in a try/catch
so a refresh failure doesn't lose the already-received response.

2. _isSendingMessage stayed true during the reconciliation fetch, keeping
   the "Thinking..." indicator visible alongside the already-displayed AI
   response and the input disabled for the entire network round-trip.

with a new _reconcileMessages helper that silently fetches messages
without touching _error, _isProviderNotConfigured, or _isLoadingMessages.
_isSendingMessage is now cleared before reconciliation begins.

fix(html): remove nested <main> — change page wrappers to <section>

All 12 pages had a <main> inside BaseLayout's <main id='main-content'>,
creating invalid nested <main> structure. Astro's ClientRouter view
transitions use DOM-level diffing that can be confused by nested landmark
elements, causing CSS/layout to break after navigation.

Fix: replace page-level <main class='...'> with <section class='...'> so
BaseLayout's single <main id='main-content'> is the only main landmark.

v10.1.1: Critical fix — unfreeze trading (3 bugs found + 1 protection)

Bug 1 (CRITICAL): Nested Smart Account creation. Wallet 0x55509 IS already
a CoinbaseSmartWallet (ERC-4337). The v10.1 code called getOrCreateSmartAccount()
which created a SECOND empty wrapper — bot read balances from the empty
wrapper ($0) instead of the real wallet ($4,628 USDC). Removed all nested
Smart Account creation; use account.swap() directly on existing SmartWallet.

Bug 2: Profit-taking capital floor logic error. When selling a position to
USDC, the check `portfolio - sellAmount < $500` incorrectly blocked sells.
Selling is value-neutral (converts tokens→USDC, portfolio stays same).
Fixed to only block when total portfolio is below floor.

Bug 3: No USDC exhaustion feedback. AI prompt had no instruction to sell
positions when USDC was depleted. Bot would hold $0 USDC forever while
profitable positions sat idle. Added rule #11 (CAPITAL RECYCLING): when
USDC < $10 and profitable positions exist, sell 20-30% of highest-gain
position to free capital.

Protection: Harvest drain prevention. Daily payout USDC buffer was $5 —
too low, causing USDC exhaustion after payouts. Raised effective reserve
to $50 minimum to keep trading operational after profit distributions.

Root cause of 3-week freeze (since Feb 15): Auto-harvest likely drained
USDC below trading threshold → no USDC for buys → no sell trigger to
recycle capital → permanent deadlock. These 4 fixes break the deadlock.
